feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: This super-cooled squirrel could revolutionise emergency care
entry.summary: No other mammal can survive colder body temperatures than the Arctic ground squirrel. Its chilly hibernation is inspiring new treatments for heart attacks, stroke, and brain injury.

entry.title: What to know about cyclosporiasis as Michigan health officials investigate 300+ cases in outbreak
entry.summary: Over 300 cases of cyclosporiasis have been reported within a week in Southeast Michigan, and Michigan health officials are investigating.

entry.title: Doctors Say There’s An Undeniable Link Between Eye Health And Dementia. Here’s What You Need To Know.
entry.summary: Just one of many reasons why routine appointments for this body part are so important.

entry.title: Vagus Nerve Stimulation Has a Profound Impact on Severe Depression, Major Trial Finds
entry.summary: Depression affects more than 300 million of us, and sometimes, treatment works.

entry.title: 5 Things to Avoid After 5 P.M. If You Have High Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Learn about the 5 things experts recommend avoiding after 5 p.m. if you have high blood pressure. Some things to be avoided include alcohol, sugary snacks and too much screen time.

entry.title: 30-Minute Dinners to Improve Your Gut Health (Weekly Plan & Shopping List!)
entry.summary: Discover quick, delicious dinners curated by a dietitian that are designed to support gut health, featuring prebiotic and probiotic-rich ingredients.

entry.title: US Life Expectancy On Track To Reach Record High
entry.summary: The US age-adjusted death rate fell to a record low in 2025, likely pushing life expectancy to a record high as overdose deaths declined and mortality improved across all age groups. CNN reports: There were about 689 deaths for every 100,000 people in the US…

entry.title: Scientists discover why some brains resist Alzheimer's
entry.summary: Some brains appear to fight back against Alzheimer's by helping immature brain cells survive damage instead of succumbing to it. Understanding this natural resilience could point researchers toward entirely new ways to protect memory and slow dementia.

entry.title: More than 60 North Carolina cases tied to parasite behind 'explosive diarrhea'
entry.summary: North Carolina is dealing with a rise in Cyclospora cases, a microscopic parasite known for unpleasant gastrointestinal symptoms, including "explosive diarrhea."

entry.title: Prehistoric Plague Could Have Caused a Population Collapse in Stone Age Europe
entry.summary: A new study reveals that plague was lethal thousands of years ago, during the Neolithic.

entry.title: Why hot dogs are (mostly) bad for you
entry.summary: Are hot dogs bad for you? In moderation, hot dogs are a fine barbecue treat, but you have to be careful about nitrates.

entry.title: A Woman Is Asked To Become Legal Guardian Of Her Schizophrenic Older Brother, And She Questions Whether She’s Ready For The Responsibility
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Don’t eat your greens? A diet taking Yemen by storm has doctors worried
entry.summary: Hospitals in Yemen report increase in workload after struck off doctor encourages diabetes patients not to take insulin.

entry.title: 7 traditional Indian foods that can naturally improve your gut health, according to nutritionist
entry.summary: There is a reason why doctors and nutritionists are talking about gut health more than ever before. The human gut is home to trillions of microorganisms that together form the gut microbiome. These tiny organisms help digest food, make certain vitamins, regul…

entry.title: 5 Everyday Foods That Keep Showing Up In Heart Doctors’ Warning Lists, And It’s Not A Coincidence
entry.summary: We all know not too eat too much salt, or sugar. But what we may not know, is that some foods that we eat daily, are actually causing our downfall. So, cardiologists compiled a short list of 5 items that we should all consider removing from out daily rota…

entry.title: How a new fungal genome-editing tool could open fresh paths to cancer treatments
entry.summary: Researchers have spent decades—and billions of dollars—sequencing animal and crop genomes, but fungi have historically been the forgotten middle child of genomics, only noticed when they're ruining bread or colonizing toes.

entry.title: I said yes to an Alzheimer’s blood test. Then I panicked.
entry.summary: The blood draw was an easy proposition. But was I ready for what the result could mean for my family and my future?

entry.title: Dangerous Tick-Spread Disease, Which Can Transmit Just 15 Minutes After Contact, Is Rising in the U.S. — Here's What to Know
entry.summary: There are no vaccines or medicines available to prevent or treat the Powassan virus

entry.title: The Connection Between Longevity and Cardio
entry.summary: The benefits of cardio go beyond getting sweaty. It brings a glow to your face, trains your heart and lungs, improves energy and mood, and even boosts longevity.

entry.title: Memory Loss May Not Be the Earliest Sign of Alzheimer’s
entry.summary: Memory Loss May Not Be the Earliest Sign of Alzheimer’s: Your cognitive flexibility may go first

entry.title: The Best Foods to Eat to Fight Inflammation, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Learn how foods can help lower inflammation in the body, plus how to incorporate more anti-inflammatory foods into your diet.

entry.title: Doctors warn that diabetic kidney damage often has no symptoms: These everyday habits can help lower the risk
entry.summary: "Take care of your body. It's the only place you have to live." This quote by author and motivational speaker Jim Rohn feels especially relevant when it comes to diabetes and kidney health.Most people living with diabetes are aware of the risks to the heart, …

entry.title: What killed Americans in 1776? The answer is dramatically different from today
entry.summary: Life expectancy rose from 30 to nearly 80 years as infectious diseases gave way to chronic conditions like heart disease, cancer and diabetes.

entry.title: What your feet are trying to tell you about your health
entry.summary: These strange or unsuspecting foot and toe symptoms can signal problems elsewhere in the body.

entry.title: Deadly drug-resistant fungal infections rising in the U.S.
entry.summary: Candida auris can “colonize” the skin and spread easily to surfaces.

entry.title: People Who Like This 1 Food Have Lower Risk of Diabetes and Hypertension, Doctors Say
entry.summary: New research links a genetic predisposition to liking onions with a lower risk of two diseases. Here, experts break it down.

entry.title: A single dose of psilocybin reduces reward-seeking behavior by altering inhibitory brain cells
entry.summary: A single dose of psilocybin reduces the drive to seek out rewards by increasing the activity of inhibitory brain cells. These findings help explain how psychedelics might induce long-lasting changes in brain circuits to treat substance use disorders.

entry.title: Some cannabis types are safer than others. What experts recommend.
entry.summary: Smoking is the most popular form of cannabis use, but it’s also the most dangerous. Here are five safer alternatives to consider.

entry.title: Old muscle stem cells can act young again but there’s a catch
entry.summary: Scientists at UCLA discovered a surprising reason aging muscles heal more slowly. In older muscle stem cells, a protein called NDRG1 builds up and acts like a brake, slowing the cells’ ability to jump into repair mode after injury. But there’s a twist: that s…

entry.title: News of disease outbreaks can warp our brains in strange and subtle ways
entry.summary: The "behavioural immune system" can drive harsher moral judgement and may even contribute to xenophobia.

entry.title: The real cause of a common stroke may have been missed for decades
entry.summary: Scientists have discovered that a common type of stroke may have a very different cause than doctors once thought. Instead of fatty plaque clogging arteries, the strongest link was found with enlarged and damaged blood vessels deep within the brain. The findi…

entry.title: ‘Explosive diarrhea parasite’ sickens 170 in Michigan, hospitalizes at least 20 nationwide
entry.summary: Cyclosporiasis, a parasitic infection that causes “explosive” watery diarrhea, has sickened dozens of people in Michigan and other parts of the United States.

entry.title: 2,000 gallons of sewage spill into Laguna Beach waters, prompting closure
entry.summary: Health officials have issued an ocean water closure at Thalia Beach in the city of Laguna Beach following a 2,000-gallon sewage spill. On July 2, around 2,000 gallons of sewage spilled into the waters due to a malfunction of a force main sewer line pump, the …

entry.title: Stronger than Ozempic. Not exactly legal. ‘Reta’ has entered the chat.
entry.summary: People are buying retatrutide online to lose weight. Is it safe? Maybe. Popular? Oh, yes.

entry.title: Modern life may be outpacing the human mind
entry.summary: A new conceptual review by researchers from the Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD) and James Cook University, Singapore, suggests that stress, loneliness and constant comparison may be linked to a mismatch between human instincts and modern …

entry.title: Pharmacist warns of heat risks for people on common medications
entry.summary: An Adel pharmacist explains how extreme heat can affect people taking certain medications and offers tips to stay safe.

entry.title: Legionnaires’ disease outbreak in 2 NYC neighborhoods puts health officials on high alert
entry.summary: Two people were diagnosed with the potentially fatal pneumonia-like illness in the neighborhoods of Carnegie Hill and Yorkville as of Thursday, with possible additional cases pending test results.

entry.title: Inland Empire city latest in SoCal to detect West Nile virus in mosquito samples
entry.summary: San Bernardino has joined a growing list of Southern California cities where mosquitoes have tested positive for West Nile virus, according to county health officials. The infected mosquitoes were collected in the city of San Bernardino, marking the first Wes…

entry.title: Former CDC official says RFK Jr.'s response to measles outbreak "not based on science or reality"
entry.summary: Dr. Debra Houry, who resigned in protest from the health agency, said the spread of misinformation impacted lives.

entry.title: Cardiologists Warn Against Combining Common Supplements with Blood Pressure Meds
entry.summary: Ironically, you might be taking it to *improve* heart health.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face in the middle of the night
entry.summary: His family was staying at a cottage in northern Ontario in 2024 when he awoke with the bat on his nose and mouth, the Canadian Medical Association Journal reported.

entry.title: Early, intense West Nile season may signal a severe year for the mosquito-borne illness, CDC says
entry.summary: According to the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the nation is seeing the earliest start to West Nile virus season in more than two decades.

entry.title: Ebola treatments trial begins in the Democratic Republic of Congo
entry.summary: According to WHO data, there have been 1,406 confirmed cases of the disease in DRC, with 301 suspected cases and 438 deaths.

entry.title: US death rate at record low: CDC data
entry.summary: The U.S. death rate fell to a record low in 2025, according to provisional data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) released Thursday. According to the Vital Statistics Rapid Release report, the death rate in 2025 was 689.2 per 100,000 …

entry.title: Is grey hair reversible? Scientists are putting color back into strands
entry.summary: Emerging research says that it may be possible to reverse the effects of greying hair without dye-ing for a solution.

entry.title: Lesion-covered squirrels with potentially life-threatening virus popping up in backyards across US
entry.summary: Squirrels covered in wart-like growths are showing up in backyards in several states with a virus that can be fatal in some cases.

entry.title: Quality of Muscles, Not Size, Lowers Heart Attack Risk
entry.summary: People with denser chest, back muscles had a much lower risk, researchers say

entry.title: You Gotta Get Up to Get Your Cancer Risk Down, New Study Finds
entry.summary: New research suggests prolonged periods of sedentary behavior are uniquely harmful to our health.

entry.title: Medicaid to drop GLP-1 coverage for tens of thousands in Mass., including children
entry.summary: Roughly 22,000 Massachusetts residents rely on Medicaid to cover anti-obesity drugs for weight loss alone.

entry.title: Tick season is expected to be worse than normal as ER visits rise in much of the U.S.
entry.summary: The CDC is reporting the highest rate of emergency room visits from tick bites since 2017 in many parts of the U.S.
